title: rhododendron × pennsylvanicum
intro: Rhododendron × pennsylvanicum, also known as the Pennsylvanic rhododendron or Great Laurel, is a hybrid of the rhododendron plant species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Rhododendron × pennsylvanicum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of various infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, have been traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
